headline: The PS6 sure sounds like a handheld
summary: Sony's next console, the PS6, is reportedly being conceived as a handheld device. The gaming industry faces turmoil with rising prices and Sony's move to kill physical game discs. Microsoft is also adjusting its console strategy, indicating a shift in the market.

headline: Starlink’s V5 dish is now available — here’s how it compares
summary: SpaceX has launched its Starlink V5 residential dish, now available in select areas. The V5 dish is significantly smaller, lighter, and more power-efficient than the V4 model. It is designed for stationary use and will see wider availability as production increases.

headline: Google and Epic give up fighting — third-party Android app stores are coming next week
summary: Google and Epic Games have settled their major lawsuit regarding Android app store policies. This agreement forces Google to allow and host rival third-party app stores within its own ecosystem. The change, impacting the US market, is expected to roll out as early as next week.

headline: Nothing’s good-looking Watch 3 Pro smartwatch is just $69
summary: The CMF by Nothing Watch 3 Pro smartwatch is currently on sale for only $69 at Amazon. This budget-friendly device features a vibrant 1.43-inch OLED display. The discounted price is a notable drop from its usual $79-$99 range.

headline: Filming Begins on The Lord of the Rings: The Hunt for Gollum, With First Look Video Showing Andy Serkis Back in Character
summary: Production has officially commenced for "The Lord of the Rings: The Hunt for Gollum." Director and star Andy Serkis has released a first-look video confirming his return as Gollum. This marks Serkis's highly anticipated comeback to Middle-earth.

headline: George Lucas Has Upset Some Fans for Saying Rejecting AI Is Like Rejecting Cars in Favor of the Horse and Buggy, but Others Aren't Surprised by His Take at All
summary: George Lucas sparked controversy by comparing rejecting AI in filmmaking to rejecting cars for horse-drawn buggies. His comments have divided fans and professionals within the Hollywood community. Lucas views AI as an inevitable technological evolution, akin to past industry shifts.

headline: New Free Cars, Leaderboard Wipes Coming to Forza Horizon 6
summary: Forza Horizon 6 is set to introduce new Italian exotic cars to the game, available for free. Playground Games is also initiating a reset of exploited leaderboards to ensure fair play. These updates aim to refresh content and enhance competitive integrity within the game.

headline: The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu Review
summary: The Mound: Omen of Cthulhu is reviewed as an entertaining co-op horror experience. While engaging, the game's atmosphere can sometimes feel excessively oppressive. It offers a unique take on the Cthulhu mythos in a cooperative gameplay setting.

headline: Midnight social media curfew proposed for UK teens aged 16 and 17 - but they can opt out
summary: The UK government is proposing a midnight social media curfew for teenagers aged 16 and 17. Critically, these teens would be able to opt out of the proposed curfew. The plan also suggests disabling 'addictive' app features like auto-play and infinite scroll.

headline: Trump threatens to bomb bridges and power plants unless Iran resumes talks
summary: Donald Trump threatened to bomb Iranian bridges and power plants if Iran does not resume talks. The warning came as the two countries exchanged fire for the fourth consecutive day. The US has also resumed blockading Iranian ports, escalating tensions.

headline: I still feel pain of '98 but this can be different for England, says Shearer
summary: Alan Shearer admits he still feels the 'pain' of England's 1998 World Cup defeat against Argentina. However, he believes the current England team has the potential to achieve a different and more successful outcome. Shearer emphasizes that this team has a unique opportunity to change their lives and make history.

headline: Review of MP security needed after Widdecombe death, Burnham says
summary: Andy Burnham has called for an urgent review of MP security following the death of Ann Widdecombe. He noted a significant 'darkening' of the political landscape in the decade since he left Westminster. The call highlights growing concerns about the safety of politicians in the UK.
